preamble: The voiced labio-velar approximant /w/ can undergo a historical phonetic transformation into the voiced dental fricative /ð/. This shift involves both a change in the manner of articulation, from an approximant to a fricative, and a significant shift in the place of articulation, from labio-velar to dental.

examples.note: Middle Welsh 'dyw' (meaning 'day') evolved to Modern Welsh 'dydd'.
Middle Welsh 'dyw' (meaning 'day') evolved to Modern Welsh 'dydd'.

sources: Weekday and month names of the British Isles. (2025). Academia.edu. (Citing Thurneysen 1901: 186).
